definition: I2S observes a specific gate-adjacent CMP literal and substitutes it into input positions, actively driving the corpus toward the matching/valid form and away from the error, malformed, or boundary state the branch requires.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace observes three byte!=0 comparisons and substitutes the constant 0 into all three payload positions, steering vpc's corpus toward the all-zeros configuration that satisfies the blocked FALSE side.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes zero constant into all three byte positions, blocking TRUE side

analysis.summary_one_line: A bKGD chunk reaching the RGB path with at least one of buf[0], buf[2], buf[4] non-zero (the invalid-color error branch).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG stream that reaches png_handle_bKGD via the non-palette, color RGB path (color_type with PNG_COLOR_MASK_COLOR set, not PALETTE) with bit_depth<=8, and supplies a 6-byte bKGD payload in which at least one of buf[0], buf[2], or buf[4] is non-zero so the disjunction (buf[0] != 0 || buf[2] != 0 || buf[4] != 0) evaluates true and the function returns via the 'invalid color' chunk-error path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile reaches this branch with the TRUE side resolved 9/10 trials. With no I2SRandReplace mutator it does not pre-load the bKGD payload bytes with the literal 0 the CMP compares against; havoc + CMP_MAP gradient feedback over the three independent !=0 compares preserves intermediate inputs whose buf[0]/buf[2]/buf[4] still hold non-zero bytes (e.g. arbitrary scratch bytes from havoc that have not been zeroed), so the disjunction trivially fires on the TRUE side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile_cmplog is identical to value_profile except for I2SRandReplace. Its cmplog observer logs the three (buf[i], 0) compares at line 2018 and the I2SRandReplace mutator substitutes the right-hand constant 0 directly into the bKGD payload positions, driving inputs toward buf[0]==0 && buf[2]==0 && buf[4]==0 — precisely the FALSE side. The blocker is FALSE (loser=8/10 blocked); I2S actively pushes payload bytes into the unique zero configuration that satisfies all three compares simultaneously, which is the harder side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Pattern B 'I2S hurts': the only technique delta between value_profile (winner) and value_profile_cmplog (loser) is I2SRandReplace. The branch's CMP shape is a 3-way disjunction of byte!=0 tests; I2S sees the constant 0 and substitutes it into the operand bytes, biasing vpc inputs toward the FALSE (blocked) side. vp, lacking I2S, retains random byte content at those offsets and trivially takes the TRUE side. The integer-distance CMP feedback that both fuzzers share is identical here, so the divergence is attributable to the I2SRandReplace substitution direction alone.

evidence_trail.claim: The branch is the three-way disjunction buf[0] != 0 || buf[2] != 0 || buf[4] != 0 at pngrutil.c:2018, inside png_handle_bKGD on the non-palette color RGB path with bit_depth<=8.
The globally blocked side is the TRUE branch — the invalid-color error path that the winner must reach.
The branch is gated by the non-palette color RGB path (color_type with PNG_COLOR_MASK_COLOR set) and bit_depth<=8.
value_profile is the winner and value_profile_cmplog is the loser at this branch under an I2S technique delta.
value_profile resolved 9/10 trials at this branch; value_profile_cmplog only 2/10 and was blocked 8/10.
The loser was blocked on average 10.25h vs the winner's 1.90h — large duration divergence, separation is significant (p_AUC=0.0004).
The DIVERGENT BRANCHES section shows W takes the TRUE side 10/20 visits at line 2018 while L takes TRUE 0/10 — direct evidence the loser never resolves while the winner does.
value_profile_cmplog is the only involved fuzzer carrying I2SRandReplace; value_profile does NOT carry it.
value_profile_cmplog's mutators are the union — havoc/token PLUS I2SRandReplace — making it the only fuzzer in this pair that can substitute the literal 0 from the CMP into the input bytes.
Both fuzzers share identical CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming-bucket on integer compares), so the CMP_MAP signal cannot explain the divergence — only the I2SRandReplace presence/absence does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Instrumentation**: union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,

evidence_trail.claim: The BYTE DIFF inside the first 64-byte window shows the bKGD payload offsets are NOT in the window (the differing offsets are 0x19, 0x35-0x38 — IHDR color_type and the chunk-name region for sRGB vs cHRM/etc.), consistent with the bKGD chunk lying further into the file where the seeds diverge structurally.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same 3-way buf[i]!=0 disjunction over a bKGD-like 6-byte payload, swept across input lengths/positions, where value_profile_cmplog matches or beats value_profile at resolving the TRUE side. If I2S being present does not actively reduce TRUE-side resolution probability at this CMP shape, the Pattern-B I2S-hurts attribution is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The W-seeds in the DB are all tagged fuzzer=cmplog (a reference fuzzer here), so we have no direct seed-byte sample from the actual decisive winner (value_profile); the mechanism attribution leans on the canonical one-technique-delta semantics (vp vs vpc differ only in I2SRandReplace) and on observed Pattern-B trial counts, not on direct byte-level evidence from vp seeds.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace observes the full CDATA literal chain and keeps re-substituting 'D' and surrounding bytes into mutated children, creating a one-way attractor toward the matched TRUE side and stranding vpc away from the needed FALSE-side non-D byte.
signature.one_line: I2S attractor substitutes CDATA literal back, preventing non-D byte diversity

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must reach the <![C prefix gate with a 5th byte that is NOT 'D' (i.e., <![C? where ? != 'D'), avoiding the CDATA literal lock-in.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Inputs whose XML_PARSER_CONTENT byte stream reaches `<![C` (cur[0..3] = '<','!','[','C') but whose 5th byte (cur[4]) is anything other than ASCII 'D' (0x44), so the AND-chain breaks at line 11750 and the parser falls through to the avail<9 / cur=='<' fallback branches instead of entering XML_PARSER_CDATA_SECTION.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile's mutator stack is havoc + tokens only (no I2SRandReplace), so when its corpus contains seeds with `<![C?` style content (or any byte sequence near the gate), random byte mutations on the 5th position have P(non-'D') = 255/256 — they freely break the 'D' byte and take the F branch. DIVERGENT BRANCHES at L11750 records W: T=0 F=38 and L: T=19 F=0, confirming the winner reaches the gate site 38 times and always falls through F while the loser always matches T.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which observes the runtime CMP `cur[4] == 0x44` (and the surrounding `cur[2]=='[', cur[3]=='C', cur[5]=='A', cur[6]=='T', cur[7]=='A', cur[8]='['` chain) and writes operand 0x44 plus the full `<![CDATA[` literal into its substitution dictionary. Once a seed in the corpus matches the CDATA prefix and takes T, I2SRandReplace keeps re-substituting these literals into mutated children — corpus drifts toward CDATA-matching inputs, and any havoc mutation that would break the 'D' byte is repeatedly 'fixed' back. The loser then descends into XML_PARSER_CDATA_SECTION (lines 11755-11757, all marked [L]) and gets stuck there processing CDATA content, never returning to encounter a non-'D' 5th byte at this gate.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: This is a Pattern-B / I2S-hurts divergence with the I2SRandReplace mutator as the technique axis. The loser (value_profile_cmplog) carries I2SRandReplace, which observes the runtime equality CMPs against the literal bytes of `<![CDATA[` and pollutes the corpus with seeds matching that prefix; once seeds matching T accumulate, mutations that would break the 'D' byte are repeatedly re-substituted back, creating a one-way attractor. The winner (value_profile) lacks I2SRandReplace, so its corpus retains diversity at offset cur[4]; havoc/token mutations on any seed reaching the `<![C` prefix have P(non-'D') = 255/256 per byte, and the F branch is taken naturally. The technique axis IS I2S; the direction of effect is reversed compared to the canonical 'I2S-helps' Pattern-A case.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a single-byte equality CMP `cur[4] == 'D'` inside the <![CDATA[ literal AND-chain at parser.c line 11750, with the false branch globally blocked.
value_profile resolves the F side in 9/10 trials while value_profile_cmplog blocks in 9/10, the decisive pair carrying the I2S technique delta.
The loser actually reaches the gate site and always matches the T branch (matches 'D'); the winner reaches it 38 times and always takes the F branch (non-'D').
The loser descends into XML_PARSER_CDATA_SECTION (SKIP(9); instate change; break) — those lines are L-only in the per-role overlay, confirming the loser commits to the CDATA path.
The winner instead takes the avail<9 fallback branch at line 11760 (goto done) — that line is W-only.
value_profile's mutator stack does NOT include I2SRandReplace, so it cannot substitute literal constants from CMP instrumentation.
value_profile_cmplog DOES include I2SRandReplace on top of vp's mutators, so it observes runtime CMP operands and substitutes them into mutated children.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a `<![CDATA[`-style 9-byte equality-AND chain on bytes parsed from input, seeded with a CDATA-matching corpus, where value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile on resolving the F branch of the 5th-byte gate. If I2S corpus pollution is the mechanism, vpc should be slower; if vpc wins or ties, the lock-in story is wrong (e.g., I2S substitutions at this gate-chain do not actually accumulate in the corpus, or CMP_MAP gradient meaningfully helps vpc explore non-D 5th bytes).

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The story relies on inferring corpus pollution as the loser's failure mode without directly inspecting whether vpc's corpus actually accumulates CDATA-prefix seeds over time — the dash-row I2S-floor signal in the L lineage confirms I2S fired somewhere in the ancestry, but does not prove that the specific substitution that pushed the seed to match the CDATA prefix is what caused the lock-in (vs. the seed being a havoc descendant of an initial seed that already contained the CDATA literal).

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts scheme/header strcmp CMPs and substitutes well-formed operands into the input, actively repairing percent-escapes that naive's random havoc would leave malformed, steering cmplog away from the decode-failure state the branch requires.
signature.one_line: I2S repairs malformed percent-escapes, steering away from decode-failure branch

analysis.summary_one_line: User field must contain a percent-escape that Curl_urldecode rejects (invalid hex or REJECT_CTRL/ZERO violation) for the protocol-handler flags active on the parsed scheme.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: After the 6-byte fixed header the input must carry a scheme tag (e.g. 'SM' for SMTP, 'pop3') that selects a handler with PROTOPT_USERPWDCTRL or similar strict decode mode, AND a USER subfield containing a malformed percent-escape (e.g. '%&', '%F', '%00' under REJECT_ZERO, or a control-byte after %-decode) so that Curl_urldecode returns non-zero and result becomes truthy at line 2102.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Naive (winner) seeds carry 'SM' and 'pop3' scheme bytes at offsets 0x06-0x09 followed by user fields packed with raw '%' literals and ASCII filler that occasionally decode to a rejected sequence (e.g. '%&%' or '%00000000'); pure random havoc on bytes 0x08-0x1f routinely produces the required malformed percent pattern because no operand-guided substitution pulls those bytes toward well-formed hex.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog (loser) executes the same havoc stack but its I2SRandReplace pass intercepts the cascade of scheme/header strcasecompare CMPs (http, ftp, ...) and substitutes those operands into the input, biasing offsets 0x06-0x0f toward well-formed scheme literals and percent-encoded operand fragments that Curl_urldecode accepts; cmplog therefore steers seeds AWAY from the brittle decode-failure state — confirmed by the loser-blocking seeds dominating offsets 0x07-0x1c with '%' literals interleaved with valid hex digits and '@'/'.' tokens characteristic of CMP-table substitution.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: havoc-only — pure random byte mutation (naive's havoc/token stack) is more effective than cmplog at producing the malformed-percent decode-failure state because I2S substitution actively REPAIRS percent-escapes by replacing them with intercepted CMP operands; this is a Pattern-B inversion where the I2S technique hurts the search.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is if(result) at line 2102 inside parseurlandfillconn, blocked on the TRUE side; result is the return value of Curl_urldecode applied to the URL's user field.
Curl_urldecode's rejection mode depends on the parsed scheme's handler flags (PROTOPT_USERPWDCTRL toggles REJECT_ZERO vs REJECT_CTRL).
The decisive pair is naive>cmplog with delta I2S; naive resolved 8/10 trials, cmplog only 2/10, confirming I2S is the technique axis that hurts here.
Winner-resolving seeds carry distinctive scheme prefixes 'SMa' and 'pop3' at offsets 0x06-0x09 followed by '%' patterns.
Another winner seed uses pop3 as the scheme tag with %00000 padding and additional '%' escapes in the user field.
Loser-blocking seeds occupy a different byte distribution at the same offsets, with '%' interleaved with hex digits and '@'/'.' tokens characteristic of CMP-operand substitution.
Per-offset byte distribution at 0x06 shows W concentrating on 'S'/'p' (SMTP/pop3) while L spreads across 'T','v','%','g' — the scheme byte is sharply separated between the two roles.

Offsets 0x0e-0x17 in the winner are concentrated on the literal '0' (0x30) byte four times — a run of ASCII zeros following a percent — whereas the loser side at the same offsets is dominated by '%' (0x25) intermixed with diverse operand fragments, indicating cmplog substitutes CMP operands into bytes naive keeps as zero-fill.

Cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets — exactly the substitution that would dilute the malformed-percent state.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Naive's mutator stack is pure havoc plus tokens with no CMP introspection, so it samples raw byte distributions uniformly and is unbiased by intercepted-operand pull.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: The per-function hit-count divergence shows cmplog (L) repeatedly enters downstream connection-setup functions (ConnectionExists, prune_dead_connections, idnconvert_hostname) while naive (W) does not — consistent with cmplog seeds passing the user-decode check and proceeding deeper, exactly what taking the FALSE side of the blocker implies.

Downstream divergent branches in create_conn (depth-2 caller) consistently show L=10 explored while W=4, confirming the loser proceeds past the decode-failure check and enters proxy/SSL/connection setup that the winner short-circuits via 'return result' at line 2103.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=2   L3722  T=4 F=0  T=0 F=10  if(result)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness reproduces the scheme-gated REJECT_ZERO/REJECT_CTRL decode-failure structure and cmplog's win rate equals or exceeds naive's at any chain length (i.e., I2S substitution does NOT bias offsets toward well-formed percent-hex), then the I2S-hurts mechanism is wrong and the divergence must come from something other than CMP-operand pull.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I infer that cmplog's I2S substitution targets scheme/header strcasecompare operands and thereby repairs malformed percent-escapes, but I have no direct CmpLogObserver dump showing which operands fired at offsets 0x07-0x1c during the loser trials — the bias is argued from the byte-distribution shift alone.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S sees chunk-magic and CRC CMPs but not the runtime-computed prefix_length; satisfying those well-formed chunk literals steers inputs toward valid keywords (length 1-79), actively avoiding the out-of-range condition the branch checks.
signature.one_line: I2S satisfies chunk magic CMPs, keeping keyword length inside valid range and blocking the malformed-length branch

analysis.summary_one_line: An iTXt chunk whose keyword field is either empty (first byte NUL) or runs more than 79 bytes before the first NUL terminator.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG input that reaches an iTXt chunk in which the keyword prefix scan computes prefix_length > 79 OR prefix_length < 1. Concretely: an iTXt chunk payload whose first byte is 0x00 (empty keyword) or whose first 80+ bytes contain no 0x00 terminator (over-long keyword).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Naive uses pure random byte mutation (byte/word/dword random/interesting/flip/inc, bytes insert/delete). Random byte placement makes it easy to either set the first iTXt payload byte to 0 (length=0) or to fill 80+ bytes of the iTXt payload with non-zero bytes (length>79). Naive resolves the branch in 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes input bytes with operands seen at intercepted CMPs (PNG chunk-type magic numbers like 'IHDR'/'iTXt', length fields, CRC operands). These substitutions push the input toward well-formed PNG keyword structures (printable ASCII followed by a NUL within 1-79 bytes), keeping prefix_length inside the valid 1..79 range. Cmplog reaches png_handle_iTXt MUCH more often than naive (HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE shows ~10-20x more invocations) yet resolves the branch in only 1/10 trials — the I2S bias steers AROUND the malformed-keyword condition rather than toward it.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Pattern-B: I2SRandReplace (cmplog's distinguishing technique vs naive) HURTS here. The branch operand prefix_length is a runtime-computed loop-count, not a literal that any CMP records, so I2S has no direct lever to set it. Meanwhile the CMPs I2S DOES see (chunk magic 'IHDR'/'iTXt', CRC, fixed-format header fields) are constraints whose satisfaction produces well-formed keywords, which fall in the [1,79] range the branch checks AGAINST. Naive's unbiased random mutation explores the malformed boundary cases more readily.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a compound length-range check on a runtime-computed keyword length, not a direct equality CMP on input bytes.
prefix_length is computed by a NUL-scan loop over the iTXt chunk buffer; it is not an input field that any CMP intercepts as a literal operand.
Naive resolves this branch 8/10 trials while cmplog resolves only 1/10 — the I2S-augmented fuzzer is strictly worse here.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive                            8        2          0  winner (I2S vs cmplog)
cmplog                           1        9          0  loser (I2S vs naive)

evidence_trail.claim: The naive>cmplog separation is statistically strong at subject level (p_AUC=0.0002, p_final=0.0002).

Cmplog (L) hits the PNG-parsing call chain dramatically more often than naive (W) — e.g. png_get_uint_31 fires 214 times for L vs 14 for W, png_check_chunk_name 192 vs 12 — yet still fails to reach the resolved side. This means cmplog is spending its budget re-parsing well-formed PNGs rather than producing the malformed iTXt keyword.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
W hits    L hits  function  (file:start-end)
      14       214  OSS_FUZZ_png_get_uint_31  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:23-30)
      17       205  OSS_FUZZ_png_crc_read  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:197-203)
      12       194  OSS_FUZZ_png_read_chunk_header  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:157-192)
      12       192  OSS_FUZZ_png_check_chunk_name  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:3136-3151)

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker itself, cmplog (L) DOES occasionally take the TRUE side (3/12) but more often takes FALSE (9/12 and 12/12 across two branch records), while naive (W) shows T=1/3 and T=1/4 — both sparse, but naive arrives with less of the auxiliary FALSE-side noise. Cmplog explores the FALSE-side downstream code (lines 2780-2850 marked [L]) extensively, which is precisely the well-formed-keyword path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
d=1   L2767  T=1 F=2  T=3 F=9  if (prefix_length > 79 || prefix_length < 1)  <-- BLOCKER
  d=1   L2767  T=1 F=3  T=0 F=12  if (prefix_length > 79 || prefix_length < 1)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: I2SRandReplace is available to cmplog and substitutes intercepted CMP operands (chunk magic, length fields, CRC operands) into the input, biasing toward well-formed PNG structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Naive's mutator stack is purely structure-agnostic random byte/word/dword/bits transforms with no CMP introspection, so its output distribution over byte sequences is unbiased relative to PNG well-formedness.

The rare cmplog winner's lineage does contain I2S-floor dash rows, confirming I2S DID fire — but it produced a win only 1/10 times because most I2S firings push the input toward well-formed PNG and away from the malformed-keyword condition.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=8d7db84a6b1ec8bc, size=8759 bytes, fuzzer=cmplog, trial=4, discovered_at=7348s, mutation_op=BytesDeleteMutator,ByteRandMutator,WordInterestingMutator,ByteInterestingMutator,ByteInterestingMutator,TokenInsert,ByteAddMutator)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the gate operand is a length scanned from a NUL-terminated input field embedded behind a PNG-style magic-number prelude should show naive > cmplog when the gate boundary lies OUTSIDE the range I2S substitutions would naturally produce (length < 1 or > 79). If, instead, a synthetic with this exact shape shows cmplog >= naive (or no separation), the 'I2S bias steers AWAY from malformed boundary' mechanism is refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The claim that I2S substitutions actively bias cmplog toward valid keyword lengths (rather than just spending budget on other parsing work) is inferential — we observe the HIT-COUNT divergence and infer the mechanism, but we did not directly trace which I2S substitutions wrote which bytes into the keyword field of any specific cmplog input.
